zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Randall Sr.於1606年12月21日出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴斯時，他的父親Lord Mayor Matthew Randall35歲，母親Agnes Cullen35歲。他於1659年3月6日與Elizabeth Irons在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1713年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福享年107歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Randall Sr.于1606年12月21日出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特巴斯时，他的父亲Lord Mayor Matthew Randall35岁，母亲Agnes Cullen35岁。他于1659年3月6日与Elizabeth Irons在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1713年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福享年107岁。

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randell
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German   Randel  .

variants.name: Randon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.
English: probably a variant of Randall , with assimilation of n-l to n-n.
French (northern): from the ancient Germanic personal name Rando, a short form of names based on the element rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’.

variants.name: Randol
meanings: English: altered form of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randal
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Randal in Gulen, a compound of Old Norse runnr ‘copse, thicket, grove’ + dalr ‘valley’.

variants.name: Randles
meanings: English and Irish (Louth): variant of Randall , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland this is sometimes a variant of Reynolds .

variants.name: Randle
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German Randel or of its Bavarian variant Randl.

variants.name: Rendell
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Dorset): variant of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randel
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
South German: variant of   Rand   3.

variants.name: Randolph
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rendall
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Scottish: habitational name from Rendall (Orkney), named with a stream-name of uncertain origin + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Swedish   Rendahl  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Randall Sr. was born on 21 December 1606, in Bath, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Lord Mayor Matthew Randall, was 35 and his mother, Agnes Cullen, was 35. He married Elizabeth Irons on 6 March 1659, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1713, in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 107.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Randall Sr. è nato il il 21 dicembre 1606 nel Bath, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Lord Mayor Matthew Randall, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Cullen, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Irons il 6 marzo 1659, nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1713, nel Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, all'età di 107 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Randall Sr.が1606年12月21日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトバスで生まれた時，父Lord Mayor Matthew Randallは35歳，母Agnes Cullenは35歳であった。1659年3月6日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンでElizabeth Ironsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1713年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアドバーで107歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Randall Sr.(이)가 1606년 12월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Mayor Matthew Randall(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Cullen(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1659년 3월 6일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 Elizabeth Irons(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1713년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 도버에서 107세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Randall Sr. nasceu em 21 de dezembro de 1606, em Bath, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Lord Mayor Matthew Randall, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Cullen, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Irons em 6 de março de 1659, em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1713, em Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 107 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Randall Sr. родился 21 декабря 1606 г. в местности бат, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Lord Mayor Matthew Randall было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Cullen, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Irons 6 марта 1659 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1713 г. в местности довир, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 107 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Randall Sr. nació el 21 de diciembre de 1606, en Bath, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Lord Mayor Matthew Randall, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Agnes Cullen, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Irons el 6 de marzo de 1659, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1713, en Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 107 años.
